title: CLAUDE.md
text: divR implements Distributional Instrumental Variable (DIV) regression R, based Holovchak, Saengkyongam, Meinshausen & Shen (2025, arXiv:2502.07641). Uses energy score-based generative modelling estimate full interventional distribution P(Y|(X)) presence unmeasured confounding. Based DistributionIV CRAN package authors (~/projects/claude/frengression/DistributionIV/), bug fixes, additional vignettes, test suite.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: cd ~/projects/software R CMD build divR --no-build-vignettes R CMD INSTALL divR_0.1.0.tar.gz

title: CLAUDE.md
text: 33 tests covering: output structure, predict dimensions (mean/sample/quantile), print method, input validation, energy loss, check_input.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Two coupled generative networks sharing hidden confounder noise dimension: - gen_g: X = g(Z, eps_x, eps_h) — treatment given instruments - gen_f: Y = f(X, eps_y, eps_h) — outcome given treatment Noise order training prediction must match: [input, eps_specific, eps_h, (W)].

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/divR.R — Main function input validation standardization R/divRfit.R — Internal fitting (torch training loop, network construction) R/predict.divR.R — S3 predict method (mean, sample, quantile trim/drop) R/print.divR.R — S3 print method R/nnmodel.R, R/energyloss.R — Shared utilities DistributionIV

title: CLAUDE.md
text: 7 vignettes reproducing paper: Sections 3.3, 5.1–5.4 (simulations), 6.1–6.2 (real data). Render: Rscript -e "rmarkdown::render('vignettes/Section5_1.Rmd')" Use cache: false torch-dependent chunks.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: checkmate (Imports) — input validation torch (Suggests) — neural network backend; requires torch::install_torch() libtorch

title: Distributional Instrumental Variable Regression
text: Distributional instrumental variable regression R.

title: Distributional Instrumental Variable Regression
text: divR implements Distributional Instrumental Variable method Holovchak, Saengkyongam, Meinshausen, Shen (2025). target interventional distribution P(Y | (X)) treatment endogenous instrument available. distribution fit, one can report means, quantiles, simulated draws. package based DistributionIV CRAN package authors. added vignettes paper simulations applications.

title: Distributional Instrumental Variable Regression
code: # Install dependencies install.packages(c("checkmate", "torch")) torch::install_torch()  # Install divR remotes::install_github("xiangao/divR")

title: Distributional Instrumental Variable Regression
code: library(divR)  # Simulate data with hidden confounding set.seed(42) n <- 1000 H <- rnorm(n) Z <- matrix(rnorm(n), ncol = 1) X <- matrix(Z + H + rnorm(n), ncol = 1) Y <- matrix(2 * X + H + rnorm(n), ncol = 1)  # Fit DIV model model <- divR(Z = Z, X = X, Y = Y, num_epochs = 500, silent = TRUE) print(model)  # Interventional prediction E[Y | do(X)] Xtest <- matrix(seq(-3, 3, length.out = 100), ncol = 1) pred_mean <- predict(model, Xtest = Xtest, type = "mean") pred_q <- predict(model, Xtest = Xtest, type = "quantile", quantiles = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9)) pred_s <- predict(model, Xtest = Xtest, type = "sample", nsample = 100)

title: Distributional Instrumental Variable Regression
text: Full documentation: https://xiangao.github.io/divR/

title: Distributional Instrumental Variable Regression
text: Section 3.3: Softplus Example — Motivating example distributional overlap density comparisons Section 5.1: Sinusoidal DGP — Nonlinear post-additive noise outcome model Section 5.2: Binary Treatment & QTE — Quantile treatment effects logistic DGP Section 5.3: Multivariate X — Multiple endogenous variables partial interventional mean Section 5.4: Instrument Strength — DIV robustness weak instruments (alpha=0 vs alpha=2)

title: Distributional Instrumental Variable Regression
text: Section 6.1: Colonial Origins — Acemoglu et al. (2001), DIV vs 2SLS vs OLS Section 6.2: Single-Cell Biology — Sachs et al. (2005) protein signaling generalizability

title: Distributional Instrumental Variable Regression
text: Holovchak, ., Saengkyongam, S., Meinshausen, N., & Shen, X. (2025). Distributional Instrumental Variable Method. arXiv:2502.07641. Shen, X. & Meinshausen, N. (2024). Engression: Extrapolation Lens Distributional Regression. JMLR.

title: Distributional IV Model Function — divR
text: function fits distributional IV model data. allows tuning several parameters related model complexity model training. Variables per default internally standardized (predictions original scale).

title: Distributional IV Model Function — divR
code: divR(   Z,   X,   Y,   W = NULL,   epsx_dim = 50,   epsy_dim = 50,   epsh_dim = 50,   hidden_dim = 100,   num_layer = 3,   num_epochs = 1000,   lr = 10^(-3),   beta = 1,   silent = FALSE,   standardize = TRUE )

title: Distributional IV Model Function — divR
text: Z data frame, matrix, vector, factor variable representing instrumental variable. X data frame, matrix, vector, factor variable representing predictor. Y data frame, matrix, vector, factor variable representing target variable. W (Optional) data frame, matrix, vector, factor variable representing exogenous variable(s). epsx_dim dimension noise corresponding predictor introduced model (default: 50). epsy_dim dimension noise corresponding outcome introduced model (default: 50). epsh_dim dimension noise corresponding hidden confounder introduced model (default: 50). hidden_dim size hidden layer model (default: 100). num_layer number layers model (default: 3). num_epochs number epochs used training (default: 1000). lr learning rate used training (default: 10^-3). beta beta scaling factor energy loss, numeric value (0,2) (default: 1). silent boolean indicating whether suppress output model training (default: FALSE). standardize boolean indicating whether standardize input data (default: TRUE).

title: Distributional IV Model Function — divR
text: distributional IV model object class 'divR'.

what: Distributional IV Model Fit Function — divRfit
title: Distributional IV Model Fit Function — divRfit
text: function fits joint distributional IV model provided data. allows tuning several parameters related model complexity model training.

code: divRfit(   Z,   X,   Y,   W,   epsx_dim = 50,   epsy_dim = 50,   epsh_dim = 50,   hidden_dim = 100,   num_layer = 3,   num_epochs = 1000,   lr = 10^(-3),   beta = 1,   silent = FALSE )

title: Distributional IV Model Fit Function — divRfit
text: Z data frame, matrix, vector, factor variable representing instrumental variable. X data frame, matrix, vector, factor variable representing predictor. Y data frame, matrix, vector, factor variable representing target variable. W (Optional) data frame, matrix, vector, factor variable representing exogenous variable(s). epsx_dim dimension noise corresponding predictor introduced model (default: 50). epsy_dim dimension noise corresponding outcome introduced model (default: 50). epsh_dim dimension noise corresponding hidden confounder introduced model (default: 50). hidden_dim size hidden layer model (default: 100). num_layer number layers model (default: 3). num_epochs number epochs used training (default: 1000). lr learning rate used training (default: 10^-3). beta beta scaling factor energy loss, numeric value (0,2) (default: 1). silent boolean indicating whether suppress output model training (default: FALSE).

title: Distributional IV Model Fit Function — divRfit
text: list containing trained DIV model matrix loss values.

title: Energy Loss Calculation — energyloss
text: function calculates energy loss given tensors. loss calculated mean L2 norms `x0` `x` `x0` `xp`, subtracted half mean L2 norm `x` `xp`.

title: Energy Loss Calculation — energyloss
Energy Loss Calculation — energyloss
text: x0 tensor representing target values. x tensor representing model's stochastic predictions. xp tensor representing another draw model's stochastic predictions. verbose boolean indicating whether return prediction loss s1 = E(||x0-x||) variance loss s2 = E(||x-xp||).

title: Energy Loss Calculation — energyloss
text: vector containing calculated energy loss, `s1`, `s2`, scalar representing calculated energy loss.

what: Energy Loss Calculation with Beta Scaling — energylossbeta
title: Energy Loss Calculation with Beta Scaling — energylossbeta
text: function calculates energy loss given tensors. loss calculated mean L2 norms `x0` `x` `x0` `xp`, raised power `beta`, subtracted half mean L2 norm `x` `xp`, also raised power `beta`.

title: Energy Loss Calculation with Beta Scaling — energylossbeta
code: energylossbeta(x0, x, xp, beta, verbose = FALSE)

title: Energy Loss Calculation with Beta Scaling — energylossbeta
text: x0 tensor representing target values. x tensor representing model's stochastic predictions. xp tensor representing another draw model's stochastic predictions. beta numeric value scaling energy loss. verbose boolean indicating whether return prediction loss s1 = E(||x0-x||) variance loss s2 = E(||x-xp||).

title: Energy Loss Calculation with Beta Scaling — energylossbeta
text: scalar representing calculated energy loss.

what: Prediction Function for a divR Model Object — predict.divR
title: Prediction Function for a divR Model Object — predict.divR
text: function computes predictions trained divR model. allows estimation interventional mean quantiles, well sampling fitted interventional distribution.

title: Prediction Function for a divR Model Object — predict.divR
code: # S3 method for class 'divR' predict(   object,   Xtest,   Wtest = NULL,   type = c("mean", "sample", "quantile")[1],   trim = 0.05,   quantiles = 0.1 * (1:9),   nsample = 200,   drop = TRUE,   ... )

title: Prediction Function for a divR Model Object — predict.divR
text: object trained divR model returned divR. Xtest matrix data frame representing predictors test set. Wtest matrix data frame representing exogenous predictors test set. type type prediction make: "mean", "sample", "quantile". trim proportion extreme values trim calculating mean (default: 0.05). quantiles quantiles estimate type "quantile" (default: 0.1*(1:9)). nsample number samples draw (default: 200). drop boolean indicating whether drop dimensions length 1 output (default: TRUE). ... additional arguments (currently ignored).

title: Prediction Function for a divR Model Object — predict.divR
text: vector matrix/array predictions.
